Title: Fermat's Last Theorem
Statement: For every integer n>2, there are no nonzero integers x,y,z with x^n+y^n=z^n.
Scope: Conditional executable theorem network migrated from Fermat Witness Lab v21; prime-exponent p>=5 route deeply represented.
Hypotheses: standard reductions from arbitrary exponent n>2 to the modeled prime-exponent route
Conclusions: no hypothetical primitive FLT solution in the modeled scope
TrustBoundary: Seven foundation theorem contracts remain imported.
NextTarget: Replace each provider dependency with a versioned provider certificate and rebuild the whole network.
